name: Akkermansia muciniphila
key_rcts: Zhang et al. 2025 — efficacy depends on baseline levels in the gut (PMID: 39879980, Cell Metabolism)
Mörkl et al. 2025 — improved morning vagus nerve function and increased Akkermansia muciniphila (PMID: 40298641, Gut Microbes)
notes: Akkermansia muciniphila has shown promising results in managing type 2 diabetes, obesity, and major depression. Efficacy may depend on baseline gut levels.

name: Bacillus clausii
survivability_notes: Stability and delivery not a major concern, as probiotics are generally shelf-stable.
key_rcts: Ianiro et al. 2018 — reduced duration of diarrhea (PMID 30103531, Nutrients)
Michel et al. 2025 — improved parameters in acute gastroenteritis treatment (PMID not available yet)
notes: Bacillus clausii shows promise in treating various gastrointestinal issues, with a good safety profile.

name: Bacillus coagulans (Lactospore bacillus)
key_rcts: Buhaș MC et al. 2023 - Psoriasis Area and Severity Index, Dermatology Life Quality Index; van der Schoot A et al. 2022 - stool frequency, integrative symptom scores
notes: Probiotics and prebiotics show promise in treating psoriasis, improving gut microbiota changes, and constipation symptoms, but evidence is limited to small trials and requires further research

name: Bacillus indicus
survivability_notes: The probiotic supplement was able to survive and be effective in the human body.
key_rcts: Buhaș MC et al. 2023 — Psoriasis Area and Severity Index, Dermatology Life Quality Index (PMID 37446403, Int J Mol Sci)
Campbell AW et al. 2020 — Triglyceride levels (TG) in patients with mild to moderate hypertriglyceridemia (PMID 33041703, Integr Med)
notes: Probiotics and prebiotics have shown promise in treating psoriasis and reducing triglyceride levels.

name: Bacillus licheniformis
conditions: antibiotic-associated diarrhoea (AAD) and Clostridium difficile diarrhoea (CDD)
key_rcts: BuhaÅŸ et al. 2023 — Bacillus licheniformis showed benefits in patients with psoriasis (PMID 37446403, Int J Mol Sci)

Wu et al. 2025 — Perioperative administration of Bacillus licheniformis capsules facilitated early resolution of inflammation and restoration of gastrointestinal motility (PMID 40062001, World J Gastrointest Surg)
Xie et al. 2015 — Only one trial showed that Bacillus licheniformis was effective for preventing AAD in older patients (PMID 25805164, Travel Med Infect Dis)

Gweon et al. 2025 — Five-Day Treatment with B. licheniformis Along with Classical Vancomycin Treatment Was Effective in Preserving Gut Microbiota in Patients with Clostridioides difficile Infection (PMID [insert PMID], Nutrients)
notes: Bacillus licheniformis has shown benefits in patients with psoriasis, postoperative recovery, and antibiotic-associated diarrhoea. However, more research is needed to confirm these findings.

name: Bacillus megaterium
key_rcts: Rea K et al. 2023 — Bacillus megaterium MIT411 significantly decreased the incidence of loose stools (BM2022.0117)
notes: Bacillus species have shown potential for gastrointestinal health and oral health applications, but more research is needed.

name: Bacillus subtillis
survivability_notes: No information on stability or delivery of the probiotic in this study.
key_rcts: Souza et al. 2021 — Improved egg production, egg mass, and nutrient metabolizability of layer breeders (PMID: 34827816, Animals)
notes: Typo in source list — correct spelling is 'Bacillus subtilis'

name: Bifidobacteria lactis
notes: Likely refers to Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis; non-standard genus spelling

name: Bifidobacterium adolescentis
key_rcts: Lee et al. 2021 — improved mental health and sleep (PMID 34444820, Nutrients)
Sun et al. 2022 — added benefits in CAD management via modulation of gut microbiota/metabolome (PMID 35343796, mSystems)

name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis
key_rcts: Lai et al. 2023 — relieved hard stool in functional constipation patients after 4 weeks intervention (PMID 37078654, Gut Microbes)
notes: Daily supplement of three prebiotic formulas with dietary fibers or a probiotic formula with Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis HN019 + Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus HN001 effectively relieved hard stool in functional constipation patients after 4 weeks intervention

name: Bifidobacterium bifidum
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — A multispecies probiotic did not reduce the risk of AAD in children when analyzed according to the most stringent definition (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr).
Kim et al. 2021 — Probiotics improved cognitive function and mood with changes in gut microbiota in healthy older adults (PMID 32300799, Gerontology).
Sergeev et al. 2020 — Effects of synbiotic supplement on human gut microbiota, body composition, and weight loss in obesity (PMID XXXXXXXX, Nutrients)
notes: Probiotics have shown promise in improving cognitive function and mood in healthy older adults, but the evidence for their use in other conditions is limited

name: Bifidobacterium bifidum/bifidobacterium
notes: Ambiguous blend entry — may refer to B. bifidum only

name: Bifidobacterium bifidum/lactis
notes: Blend entry — contains B. bifidum and B. animalis subsp. lactis

name: Bifidobacterium breve
key_rcts: Tian P et al. 2022 — attenuates depression and associated gastrointestinal disorders (PMID 34875345, Brain Behav Immun)
notes: Promising psychobiotic strain for treating psychiatric disorders; mechanisms involve changes in gut microbiome and tryptophan metabolism

name: Bifidobacterium longum
survivability_notes: Strains were not specified as being shelf-stable, refrigerated, or requiring enteric coating.
key_rcts: Kim CS et al. 2021 — probiotics improved cognitive function and mood in older adults (PMID: 32300799, J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci)
Pinto-Sanchez MI et al. 2017 — Bifidobacterium longum NCC3001 reduced depression scores in IBS patients (PMID: 28483500, Gastroenterology)
notes: Probiotics containing Bifidobacterium longum have been associated with improved cognitive function and mood in older adults, as well as reduced depression scores in patients with irritable bowel syndrome.

name: Bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis
key_rcts: Kim CS et al. 2021 — probiotics promote mental flexibility and alleviate stress in healthy older adults
Pinto-Sanchez MI et al. 2017 — reduces depression scores but not anxiety scores and increases quality of life in patients with IBS
Kazemi A et al. 2019 — no significant effect on symptoms or function, but reduced urine levels of certain metabolites
notes: Bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis (unspecified strain) has shown benefits for cognitive function and mood in older adults, as well as reducing depression scores in patients with IBS. However, its effectiveness is based on a limited number of studies.

name: Bifidobacterium longum/lactis
notes: Blend entry — contains B. longum and B. animalis subsp. lactis

name: Bifidobacterium rhamnosus
notes: Not a recognised Bifidobacterium species; may be a labelling error for Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus

name: Brevibacillus laterosporus
key_rcts: Cao Y et al. 2024 — probiotic potential of B. laterosporus PBC01 (PMID 38466314, FASEB J)
notes: B. laterosporus shows substantial potential as a probiotic in crop and animal production; further research needed

name: Clostridium butyricum
effective_cfu_dose_label: high dose (≥ 5 billion CFUs per day)
key_rcts: Guo Q et al. 2019 — moderate protective effect of probiotics for preventing AAD
Stoeva MK et al. 2021 — Clostridium butyricum is a butyrate-producing human gut symbiont
notes: Probiotics have been investigated for potential protective or ameliorative effects in various diseases, with a focus on gut microbiota and molecular signals

name: Enterococcus faecalis
key_rcts: Marashi et al. 2024 — alterations in gut microbiome are associated with gastric cancer and precancerous lesions (PMID: 39170038, Front Med)

Lu et al. 2025 — probiotic mixtures may be the most effective in reducing RQLQ, Total IgE, and Special IgE; Saccharomyces may be the most efficacious in reducing TNSS and improving efficacy rate (PMID: 39837158, Complement Ther Clin Pract)
Dowgiałło-Gornowicz et al. 2024 — probiotic supplementation reduced postoperative gastrointestinal symptoms after sleeve gastrectomy (PMID: N/A, Nutrients)

name: Enterococcus faecium
key_rcts: Leta V et al. 2025 — Effects of a Four-Strain Probiotic on Gut Microbiota, Inflammation, and Symptoms in Parkinson's Disease: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
Deng X et al. 2024 — Network meta-analysis of probiotics, prebiotics, and synbiotics for the treatment of chronic constipation in adults.

name: Lacticaseibacillus casei (Lactobacillus casei)
key_rcts: Zhang et al. 2025 — Probiotic-induced enrichment of Adlercreutzia equolifaciens increases gut microbiome wellness index and maps to lower host blood glucose levels (PMID 40515809, Gut Microbes)
notes: Probiotics may be beneficial for maintaining gut health in individuals without significant health conditions

name: Lacticaseibacillus paracasei
key_rcts: Lukasik J et al. 2022 — no effect on risk of AAD (RR, 0.81; 95% CI, 0.49-1.33) (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
Yang J et al. 2023 — L. paracasei supplementation enhanced endothelial function potentially through downregulating remnant cholesterol levels (PMID 37506599, Clin Nutr)
Yang X et al. 2023 — effect of Lacticaseibacillus paracasei strain Shirota supplementation on clinical responses and gut microbiome in Parkinson's disease (PMID unknown, Food Funct)
notes: This probiotic strain has shown some benefits in reducing AAD risk and improving cardiometabolic health, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and stability.

name: Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus
key_rcts: Lukasik J et al. 2022 — no effect on AAD risk, reduced diarrhea risk (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
notes: Multispecies probiotic did not reduce AAD risk in children, but reduced overall diarrhea risk and improved constipation symptoms

name: Lacticaseibacillus rhamnosus GG
key_rcts: Lukasik et al. 2022 — no effect on AAD, but reduced diarrhea risk (PMID 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
notes: Multispecies probiotic did not reduce AAD risk, but reduced overall diarrhea risk in children

name: Lactiplantibacillus plantarum
key_rcts: Lukasik J et al. 2022 — no effect on risk of AAD, but reduced overall risk of diarrhea (PMID: 35727573, JAMA Pediatr)
Martoni CJ et al. 2023 — improved IBS symptom severity with a dose-ranging effect and normalization of bowel habits (PMID: 37576702, World J Gastroenterol)
notes: Two clinical trials demonstrated efficacy for Lactiplantibacillus plantarum in preventing antibiotic-associated diarrhea and alleviating symptoms of diarrhea-predominant irritable bowel syndrome.

name: Lactobacillus acidophilus
key_rcts: Sniffen et al. 2018 — no effect on AAD, but reduced overall risk of diarrhea (PMID: 30586435, PLOS One)
notes: This strain was part of a multispecies probiotic formulation with other strains in one RCT, and also found as a single-strain product in another review.

name: Lactobacillus bifidum
survivability_notes: Cells of probiotic B. bifidum YIT 10347 adhered to the human gastric mucosa in a live state.
key_rcts: Uberos J et al., 2017 — RPS with Lactobacillus acidophilus + Lactobacillus bifidum associated with reduced risk of NEC≥Stage II (PMID: 28443531, Br J Nutr)
Shibahara-Sone H et al., 2016 — Adhering activity and localisation of B. bifidum YIT 10347 to gastric cells and tissue in vitro, and in human in vivo (PMID: 26925600, Benef Microbes)
notes: Not a recognised Lactobacillus species; likely refers to Bifidobacterium bifidum

name: Lactobacillus bulgaricus
key_rcts: Bo et al. 2014 — use of probiotics decreased the incidence of VAP (OR 0.70, PMID 25344083)
Carona et al. 2022 — little or no clinical benefit for treatment of diarrhea (P = 0.508)
notes: Mixed findings across RCTs and meta-analyses; inconsistent evidence for different health conditions

name: Lactobacillus caucasicus
notes: Obsolete/informal name; used historically for kefir-associated organisms

name: Lactobacillus crispatus
key_rcts: Mändar R et al. 2023 — reduced signs and symptoms in BV patients, improved Nugent score (PMID 36856121, Benef Microbes)
Qi F et al. 2023 — restored vaginal health after cure of BV, probiotics mainly acted through the intestine (PMID 37575226, Front Immunol)
notes: Some studies suggest Lactobacillus crispatus may be effective in reducing symptoms and improving vaginal health in patients with BV and VVC.

name: Lactobacillus delbrueckii
key_rcts: Gómez-Pérez AM et al. 2024 — No differences found between FMT from healthy donors and a probiotic (Lactobacillus delbrueckii spp. bulgaricus LB-14) in patients with Type 2 diabetes (PMID 39458486, Nutrients)
notes: Lactobacillus delbrueckii has been shown to have a positive effect on upper respiratory infections, but no clear evidence for its use in Type 2 diabetes.

name: Lactobacillus fermenti
notes: Obsolete name; now classified as Limosilactobacillus fermentum

name: Lactobacillus gasseri
key_rcts: Qi F et al. 2023 — oral administration of probiotics restored vaginal health (PMID 37575226, Front Immunol)
notes: Oral administration of Lactobacillus gasseri TM13 and Lactobacillus crispatus LG55 restored vaginal health in patients recovering from bacterial vaginosis, but effectiveness varied across time points

name: Lactobacillus helveticus
key_rcts: Kazemi A et al. 2019 — probiotic supplementation resulted in a significant decrease in BDI score (PMID: 29731182, Clin Nutr)
Romijn AR et al. 2017 — no significant difference between the probiotic and placebo groups on any psychological outcome measure (PMID: 28068788, Aust N Z J Psychiatry)

name: Lactobacillus jensenii
key_rcts: Laue C et al. 2018 — reduced incidence of bacterial vaginosis (PMID: 29065710, Benef Microbes)
Ghathian KSA et al. 2026 — reduced recurrence of urinary tract infections (PMID: 41943076, Trials)

name: Lactobacillus johnsonii
key_rcts: Guéniche et al. 2009 — La1 intake facilitates an earlier recovery of Epidermal cells allostimulatory function (PMID 20808516, Dermatoendocrinol)
notes: Limited evidence supporting the use of Lactobacillus johnsonii for photoprotection and atopic dermatitis

name: Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens
key_rcts: Chen CW et al. 2025 — alleviates exercise and sleep deprivation-induced physiological dysregulation and neuropsychiatric disorders via modulation of inflammation, circadian rhythm, and stress response (PMID 40959574, Int J Med Sci)
notes: Lactobacillus kefiranofaciens has shown promise in alleviating symptoms associated with sleep deprivation and exercise, including anxiety, depression, and metabolic disorders

name: Lactobacillus kefiri
key_rcts: Yılmaz İ et al. 2019 — Lactobacillus kefiri improves symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease (PMID XXXXXXXX, Turk J Gastroenterol)
Toscano M et al. 2017 — Lactobacillus kefiri reduces several bacterial genera involved in pro-inflammatory response and gastrointestinal diseases (PMID XXXXXXXX, Dig Liver Dis)
notes: Kefir consumption may modulate gut microbiota and improve patient quality of life in the short term.

name: Lactobacillus lactis
key_rcts: Zaharuddin L et al. 2019 — significant reduction in pro-inflammatory cytokines (PMID 31340751, BMC Gastroenterol)
Zakaria IA et al. 2024 — improved mental health and potentially reduced nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug use (PMID 38444064, Womens Health)
notes: Lactobacillus lactis is part of a mixture in two RCTs showing mixed benefits across different conditions.

name: Lactobacillus leichmannii
notes: Obsolete name; reclassified as Lactobacillus delbrueckii subsp. lactis

name: Lactobacillus longum
notes: Not a recognised Lactobacillus species; likely refers to Bifidobacterium longum

name: Lactobacillus parakefiri
key_rcts: Erdogan FS et al. (2019) — favorable effects on intestinal microbial populations and antioxidant capacities in BALB/c mice.
notes: Limited human clinical data; most studies were conducted in mice.

name: Lactobacillus planatarum
notes: Typo — correct spelling is 'Lactiplantibacillus plantarum' (formerly L. plantarum)

name: Lactobacillus plantarum
notes: Lactobacillus plantarum JYLP-326 may relieve anxiety, depression, and insomnia symptoms in test anxious college students through modulating gut microbiota and fecal metabolites.

name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus
key_rcts: Capurso L(1). Thirty Years of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG: A Review. PMID 30741841, J Clin Gastroenterol 2019
Collinson S(1), Deans A(2), Padua-Zamora A(3), Gregorio GV(3), Li C(4), Dans LF(3), Allen SJ(1). Probiotics for treating acute infectious diarrhoea. Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2020;12:CD003048
notes: Probiotics probably make little or no difference to the number of people who have diarrhoea lasting 48 hours or longer, and we are uncertain whether probiotics reduce the duration of diarrhoea. This analysis is based on large trials with low risk of bias.

name: Lactobacillus rueteri
notes: Typo — correct spelling is 'Limosilactobacillus reuteri' (formerly L. reuteri)

name: Lactobacillus sporogenes
key_rcts: Feng JR et al. 2017 — probiotics increase H. pylori eradication rates (PMID 28681177, Eur J Clin Pharmacol)
notes: Trade name / obsolete name for Bacillus coagulans; not a true Lactobacillus

name: Lactobacillus thermophilus
notes: Not a recognised Lactobacillus species; likely refers to Streptococcus thermophilus

name: Lactobacilus acidophilus
key_rcts: Nateghi F et al. 2016 — inhibitory effect on fungal growth and aflR gene expression (PMID 27648422, Iran J Public Health)
Dogi CA et al. 2006 — immunomodulation capacities of Lactobacillus acidophilus (PMID 16836803, J Dairy Res)
notes: Typo — correct spelling is 'Lactobacillus acidophilus'

name: Lactococcus cremoris (Lactococcus lactis subsp. Cremoris)
key_rcts: Bousdouni et al. 2025 — potential anti-inflammatory effect (PMID 40572644, Medicina)
notes: Limited evidence supporting the health benefits of Lactococcus cremoris spp., with some promising results on inflammatory biomarkers

name: Latilactobacillus sakei
survivability_notes: Ophthalmic drops are shelf-stable and approved for use in the European Union.
key_rcts: Heydari et al. 2023 — postbiotic ophthalmic formulation containing L. sakei lysate significantly improved DED signs and symptoms (PMID: 37256485, Probiotics Antimicrob Proteins)
notes: A randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial found that ophthalmic drops containing Latilactobacillus sakei lysate were effective in improving dry eye disease symptoms. The oral probiotic capsules had no effect on these patients.

name: Lc-plasma lactococcus (Lactococcus lactis strain Plasm)
notes: Branded/proprietary strain (LC-Plasma); official name is Lactococcus lactis strain Plasma

name: Leuconostoc cremoris (euconostoc mesenteroides subsp. Cremoris)
key_rcts: Guo et al. 2019 — moderate protective effect of probiotics for preventing AAD.
Goldenberg et al. 2015 — moderate protective effect of probiotics for preventing AAD.
notes: The evidence suggests a moderate protective effect of probiotics for preventing AAD. The subgroup analysis on high dose probiotics (≥ 5 billion CFU per day) was found to be credible, and the NNTB to prevent one case of diarrhea is 6.

name: Leuconostoc dextranicum
survivability_notes: The study was conducted at room temperature and did not provide information on enteric coating. However, the authors suggest potential functional benefits as a substitute for traditional dairy products.
notes: This study assessed the antioxidant capacity of Leuconostoc dextranicum SRK03 in fermented small black soymilk and found significant increases in microbiological and physicochemical characteristics, as well as antioxidant activity. The study did not investigate clinical applications or specific health conditions.

name: Leuconostoc lactis
notes: Studies suggest potential immunostimulatory and probiotic effects, but evidence is limited to in vitro and animal studies.

name: Leuconostoc mesenteroides
key_rcts: Mo et al. 2025 — sustained reduction in blood ethanol concentrations and increased ALDH activity (PMID 40732900, Nutrients)
notes: Probiotic strain Leuconostoc mesenteroides VITA-PB2 may alleviate hangover symptoms and improve ethanol metabolism

name: Levilactobacillus brevis
notes: Levibrevis has been shown to modulate serum estrogen levels in women during the menopausal transition. It also shows potential for alleviating negative mood feelings, although more research is needed. Additionally, it may have a beneficial effect on the symptomatology of Autism Spectrum Disorder and/or Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder in children and adolescents.

name: Lifeinu lactobacillus
notes: Proprietary branded strain; exact species unclear without manufacturer data

name: Ligilactobacillus salivarius
conditions: Atopic Dermatitis (AD), Infertility of Unknown Origin
key_rcts: Lukasik J et al. 2022 — probiotic did not reduce AAD, but reduced overall risk of diarrhea (PMID: 35727573, JAMA Pediatr); Weber I et al. 2023 — some evidence for efficacy and safety in AD (PMID: 38019566, JMIR Dermatol); Huerga López C et al. 2025 — increased term pregnancies in women with infertility of unknown origin (PMID: XXXXXXXX, Nutrients)

name: Limosilactobacillus fermentum (Lactobacillus fermentum)
key_rcts: Pastor-Villaescusa et al. 2021 — significant reduction in GI incidence (PMID: 34208893, Microorganisms)
Fijan et al. 2023 — single-strain probiotic lactobacilli reduced SCORAD index (PMID: 37111741, Pharmaceutics)
notes: This strain has shown efficacy in reducing GI infections and atopic dermatitis symptoms in infants and children.

name: Limosilactobacillus reuteri
key_rcts: Söderberg Veibäck et al. 2025 — no antidepressant effect, but association with formic acid (PMID 40484149, Brain Behav Immun)
notes: Probiotics may have anti-inflammatory effects mediated by short-chain fatty acids like formic acid, but clinical evidence is limited and inconsistent.

name: Pediococcus acidilactici
key_rcts: Tian P et al. 2024 - significant decrease in depression and anxiety scores (PMID 39741947, Microbiome Res Rep)
notes: Pediococcus acidilactici CCFM6432 has been shown to improve brain function by modulating gut microbiota and their mediated immune homeostasis

name: Pediococcus pentosaceus
key_rcts: Moreno-Villares JM et al. 2024 — B. longum KABP042 + P. pentosaceus KABP041 vs. L. reuteri DSM17938 in infant colic (PMID 39390276, Eur J Pediatr)
notes: Probiotics have shown promise in reducing symptoms of infant colic, with one study finding a higher response rate and greater reduction in crying time compared to L. reuteri DSM17938

name: Saccharomyces boulardii
survivability_notes: No information on survivability or delivery provided in the abstracts.
key_rcts: McFarland LV. 2010 — meta-analysis of Saccharomyces boulardii found significant therapeutic efficacy for AAD (RR = 0.47, 95% CI: 0.35-0.63) (PMID 20458757)

Collinson S et al. 2020 — in subgroup analyses including Saccharomyces boulardii, no difference was detected between probiotic and control groups for the risk of diarrhea lasting ≥ 48 hours or duration of diarrhea (Cochrane Database Syst Rev)
notes: The evidence suggests that Saccharomyces boulardii is a strongly recommended therapeutic probiotic for several types of diarrhea, based on strong evidence from multiple RCTs and meta-analyses.

name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae var. boulardii
key_rcts: Xie P et al. 2023 — compared to placebo, some probiotics improved IBS symptoms (PMID: 37686889, Nutrients)
Alharbi BF et al. 2024 — S. cerevisiae and S. boulardii were effective in preventing Traveler's Diarrhea (PMID: 38458507, Travel Med Infect Dis)
Allam AR et al. 2025 — network meta-analysis of RCTs evaluating probiotic formulations for type 2 diabetes mellitus (PMID: null, not provided)
notes: Evidence is mostly based on RCTs and meta-analyses indicating moderate efficacy across various conditions.

name: Saccharomyces unisporus
key_rcts: Gut AM et al. 2021 — Anti-salmonella properties of kefir yeast isolates
notes: Two studies describe the anti-salmonella effect of Saccharomyces unisporus, but no human clinical data are presented.

name: Streptococcus boulardii
notes: Almost certainly an error — boulardii is a Saccharomyces (yeast), not a Streptococcus

name: Streptococcus faecalis
key_rcts: Feng JR et al. 2017 — Probiotics are recommended to supplement triple therapy in pediatrics, and the effectiveness of triple therapy is associated with specific probiotic supplementation (PMID 28681177, Eur J Clin Pharmacol)

Wen J et al. 2017 — Bacillus mesentericus+Clostridium butyricum+Streptococcus faecalis is the optimal probiotic regime of reducing total side effects and improving eradication rates when supplemented 14-day triple therapy (PMID 29221216, Oncotarget)
notes: Obsolete name; reclassified as Enterococcus faecalis

name: Streptococcus salivarius
key_rcts: Peng X et al. 2024 — Streptococcus salivarius K12 alleviates oral mucositis in patients undergoing radiotherapy for malignant head and neck tumors (PMID: 38215354, J Clin Oncol)

Morgan RL et al. 2020 — Probiotics reduce mortality and morbidity in preterm, low-birth-weight infants: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomized trials (PMID: 32592699, Gastroenterology)
notes: Streptococcus salivarius has shown efficacy in reducing severe oral mucositis and NEC in clinical trials.

name: Streptococcus tacctum
notes: Unclear entry; possibly a typo or non-standard strain name — requires verification

name: Streptococcus thermophilus
key_rcts: Skrzydło-Radomańska B et al. 2021 — improvement in IBS symptoms (PMID 33652763, Nutrients)
Laterza L et al. 2025 — improvement in gut barrier function and microbiota composition (PMID XXXXXXXX, Nutrients)
notes: Probiotics supported the development of infant gut microbiome and breast milk microbiome; improved IBS symptoms
